title: phlebodium aureum var. areolatum
intro: Phlebodium aureum var. areolatum, also known as the gold polypody or resurrection fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Phlebodium aureum var. areolatum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential interactions or side effects.
